name: Green Tortoise Hostel Seattle
description: World-famous social hostel directly across from Pike Place Market. Bunk dorms year-round, private rooms in fall/winter. Free breakfast, communal kitchen, laundry, nightly social events (pub crawls Fridays), 24/7 staff. 30 rooms, sleeps up to 150.

name: American Hotel Hostel
description: Seattle's largest hostel in the historic Chinatown-International District, renovated 2009. Dorms and private rooms, communal kitchen, lounge spaces, laundry, library/game room, free WiFi, regular social events. Near Amtrak, Greyhound, stadiums.

name: Hostel Fish Seattle
description: Premium hostel in Seattle's Belltown neighborhood, blocks from Space Needle and Pike Place Market. Communal lounge, bunk dorm rooms, free WiFi. Front desk open 7am–3am.

name: City Hostel Seattle
description: Art-themed hostel in a renovated 1920s building in Belltown — each dorm room painted by a different local Seattle artist (47+ murals). Small on-site movie theatre, communal spaces. Between Space Needle and Pike Place Market.

name: HotelHotel Hostel
description: Small, community-focused hostel in Seattle's artsy Fremont neighborhood. Free breakfast, free WiFi, free linens, full communal kitchen, bike rentals, polaroid camera rental. Near Woodland Park Zoo and Seattle Center.

name: North Cascades Mountain Hostel
description: Adventure basecamp in downtown Winthrop, Methow Valley. Bunk dorms, private rooms, and deluxe private cabins with deck views of the Cascades. Fully stocked communal kitchens, laundry, free WiFi, dog-friendly. HI USA referral hostel. Near hiking, biking, skiing, rock climbing.

name: San Juan Island Hostel
description: 3 blocks from the ferry terminal in Friday Harbor. Shared dorm rooms, private dorms, group dorm, full communal kitchen, free WiFi, free parking. Night manager on site. Walking distance to whale watch tours, kayak rentals. No guests under 14. Max 14-night stay.

name: Wayfarer's Rest
description: Budget hostel 4 blocks from the ferry terminal in Friday Harbor, operating since 1999. Two fully equipped communal kitchens, dorm rooms from ~$30/night. NOTE: Hostelz.com previously flagged as closed — call ahead to confirm current status.

name: Golden Tree Hostel
description: Beautifully renovated 1893 pioneer farmhouse hostel on Orcas Island, Eastsound. Communal and private rooms, manicured garden, outdoor communal kitchen, pizza oven, free WiFi. 2 minutes walk to Eastsound town center. Dorm beds from ~$69.

name: Rain Forest Hostel
description: Remote hostel on 17 acres between Ruby Beach and the Hoh Rain Forest, 22 miles south of Forks on Hwy 101. Dorm bunks, full communal kitchen, deck, library, laundry. Payment by donation (~$10/person suggested), cash only. One of the most authentic, community-oriented hostels in the PNW.

name: The Society Hotel Bingen
description: Converted 1908 schoolhouse in the Columbia River Gorge. Two 24-bed bunk rooms (triple-stacked), each bunk with privacy curtain, reading light, power outlet, lockable storage. Communal spaces, underground spa with saltwater soaking pool, cold plunge, hot tub, bar & café, 20 private cabins. Budget bunk-room travelers get full spa access.

name: ToadLily House International Hostel
description: House-style international hostel in Port Angeles, gateway to Olympic National Park. Main dorm with 3 bunk beds, upstairs room for groups of 3, private rooms available. Communal kitchen, showers, laundry, free WiFi, free parking, bike parking. Pet-friendly. Doorbell rings to host's cell for late arrivals.

name: Brewster Hall
description: Hostel-style communal lodging in Cheney, WA (20 min west of Spokane). Community kitchen and lounge, free WiFi, laundry, meeting rooms, 24-hr check-in, luggage storage. Short-term and extended stays. Only budget-communal option in Eastern WA.
